title: Privacy Policy
scope: This Policy applies only to Blotz ("we", "us", or "our"), the Blotz product, and related services.
intro: The security of your personal information is important to us. We collect and use your personal information in accordance with applicable laws and regulations so we can provide better products and services. We are committed to keeping your personal information and other data confidential and secure.
Please read this Privacy Policy (the "Policy") carefully before using Blotz and related services. By using Blotz, you agree to this Policy.

sections.title: How We Collect and Use Your Personal Information

blocks.text: We follow the principles of legitimacy, lawfulness, and necessity. Based on the functions and services described below, we collect and use the personal information that you voluntarily provide while using the product and services.
You can register and sign in to your Blotz account in the following ways:

items.label: Register with email
items.text: To create your account and allow future sign-in by email, we collect the email address you provide and send a verification email to confirm it.

items.label: Register with a third-party account
items.text: If you choose to register with a third-party account such as Apple or Google, we obtain certain basic profile information from that platform, such as your nickname, avatar, and email address, in accordance with the relevant authorization agreement, so we can create your Blotz account.

blocks.text: Blotz does not offer a local-only mode without registration. You must register an account to use all Blotz features, and your task data will sync across your devices.
After you register an account and create tasks or other data, we collect the following information so your data can sync properly and completely across devices:

items: Task title, task content, priority, due date or time range, reminder time, and recurrence rules
Device information associated with editing actions, including device model, operating system version, and unique device identifier

blocks.text: We use this information only to sync your task data across devices. We do not read your task content for other purposes without your authorization.
If you set a reminder for a task in Blotz, we process the task title, task content, due date or time range, and reminder time so the app can notify you at the scheduled time.

If you set a location-based reminder for a task and grant Blotz access to location permissions, we may collect your saved location coordinates so Blotz can notify you when you arrive at or leave that location.
If you use search in Blotz, we may collect the keywords you search for so you can view recent search history. These keywords are stored only locally on your device and are not synced to the cloud.
Some Blotz features are powered by artificial intelligence. We integrate Microsoft Azure OpenAI services for those features.

When you actively use an AI feature, we process only the content you choose to enter or select, such as task titles, task details, quick notes, tags, and date information. That data is transmitted to Azure OpenAI to generate content or suggestions related to your request.
Under Microsoft's default Azure OpenAI data handling policy, your prompts and completions are not used by Microsoft to train models and are not persistently stored after the API request is completed.

blocks.label: Microsoft Azure OpenAI data privacy information

blocks.text: Your input may be quoted, rewritten, or combined in the AI output to better complete the task you requested. All such processing is limited to what is needed to provide the feature. We do not use this data for unrelated purposes or for model training.

You decide whether to use AI features. If you choose not to use them, we will not trigger related collection, processing, or third-party transfer, and your use of other core services will not be affected.

You may choose to complete your profile by providing an avatar and nickname. This information is optional and is not required for core product or system functionality. If you choose not to provide it, your use of Blotz will not be adversely affected.

Some features require system permissions that may involve personal information. We request authorization only when you use those features, and we access related information only after receiving your permission. You may refuse authorization if you do not need the feature.

items.label: Calendar access
items.text: Requested when you enable calendar sync so system calendar events can sync into Blotz.

items.label: Approximate and precise location
items.text: Requested when you use location reminders so Blotz can remind you when you arrive at or leave a selected location.

items.label: Write external storage
items.text: Requested when you save attachments from a task to your phone.

items.label: Read external storage
items.text: Requested when you upload attachments to a task.

items.label: Microphone access
items.text: Requested when you add audio attachments or use voice input.

items.label: Fingerprint access
items.text: Requested when you enable fingerprint unlock for the app.

items.label: Clipboard access
items.text: If you enable clipboard recognition and the clipboard contains time-related information, the app may ask whether you want to create a task when you open it.

items.label: Calendar access
items.text: Requested when you enable calendar sync.

items.label: Approximate and precise location
items.text: Requested when you use location reminders.

items.label: Write external storage
items.text: Requested when you save attachments from a task to your phone.

items.label: Read external storage
items.text: Requested when you upload attachments to a task.

items.label: Microphone access
items.text: Requested when you add audio attachments or use voice input.

items.label: Face ID / biometric access
items.text: Requested when you set an app unlock password and enable Face ID.

items.label: Clipboard access
items.text: If you enable clipboard recognition and the clipboard contains time-related information, the app may ask whether you want to create a task when you open it.

items.label: Siri access
items.text: Requested when you enable adding tasks through Siri.

blocks.text: Blotz uses the following third-party libraries and services to support core functionality:

items.label: Backend services (Microsoft / NuGet)
items.text: Our backend is built with Microsoft NuGet components for server-side logic, data storage, and API communication.

items.label: Frontend framework (React Native / Expo)
items.text: Our mobile app frontend is built with React Native and Expo. These frameworks themselves do not independently collect your personal information and are used to provide the app interface and interactions.

items.label: AI services (Microsoft Azure OpenAI)
items.text: As described above, we use Microsoft Azure OpenAI for AI features. The Azure OpenAI server we use is located in US East.

blocks.text: From time to time, we may conduct user research, mainly in the form of surveys. To better understand different user groups and improve our features and services, we may collect information such as age, gender, industry, occupation, device information, and personal interests from participants.
We may also run social media activities from time to time. To let users participate and receive prizes, we may collect information such as name, phone number, and mailing address.
This information is used only for the relevant activity. If you do not want to provide it, you may choose not to participate in the survey or activity.

sections.title: How We Use Cookies and Similar Technologies

blocks.text: A cookie is a small text file placed on a client device by a website server when you browse a website. When you visit the same site again, the site can recognize your browser through the cookie. Cookies may store user preferences and other information.

Blotz uses cookies as a convenience so you do not need to repeatedly verify your identity when moving between Blotz services. We use cookies to store preferences, improve user experience, and analyze service usage so we can improve service quality.
You can refuse cookies by changing your browser or device settings. If you do so, you may not be able to sign in to or use Blotz services or features that depend on cookies.

sections.title: How We Share, Transfer, and Publicly Disclose Your Personal Information

blocks.text: We do not share your personal information with other companies, organizations, or individuals without your consent, except in the following cases:

items.label: With your explicit consent or authorization
items.text: After obtaining your explicit authorization, we may share your personal information with other parties, including AI service providers such as Microsoft Azure OpenAI.

items.label: Where required by law
items.text: We may share your personal information when required by laws and regulations, for dispute resolution, or in response to mandatory requests or judicial decisions from competent authorities.

items.label: To protect legitimate interests
items.text: We may provide your personal information to third parties when necessary to protect Blotz, our users, or the public from harm to rights, property, or safety.

blocks.text: We do not transfer your personal information to any company, organization, or individual without your consent, except in the following cases:

items: With your explicit consent or authorization
In the event of a merger, acquisition, or bankruptcy liquidation, in which case we will require the new holder of your personal information to continue to be bound by this Policy

blocks.text: We do not publicly disclose your personal information without your consent, except in the following cases:

items: With your explicit consent or authorization
When required by law, legal process, litigation, or mandatory requests from competent authorities, in which case we will apply security measures that meet legal and industry standards

sections.title: How We Store Your Personal Information

blocks.text: While you use our platform, we will continue to retain your personal information for no longer than necessary to provide services to you. After you stop using the platform or withdraw relevant authorizations, we will delete or anonymize your information unless laws or regulations require a longer retention period for specific information.

Our primary servers are located in Australia. If some features involve cross-border transfers, such as AI features that use Microsoft Azure OpenAI services hosted in US East, we will comply with applicable legal requirements and protect the security of your personal information.

sections.title: How We Protect Your Personal Information

blocks.text: The security of your personal information is very important to us. We comply with applicable laws and regulations and adopt reasonable and practical industry-recognized measures to protect your personal information against unauthorized access, disclosure, use, alteration, damage, or loss.

We use security measures that align with industry standards, including SSL/TLS encryption in transit, encryption technologies to protect confidentiality, and access control mechanisms to ensure that only authorized personnel can access personal information.

If a personal information security incident occurs, we will notify you in a timely manner as required by law, including the basic situation, possible impact, measures taken or to be taken, and suggestions for your own protection. We may notify you by email, push notification, or similar means.

sections.title: Your Rights to Manage Personal Information

blocks.text: You have the right to access and correct your personal information. You can do so in the following ways:

items.label: Profile information
items.text: Go to Settings > Account to view or modify your avatar, nickname, email account, and login password.

items.label: Search history
items.text: Open search to view or clear recent search keywords.

blocks.text: You may request deletion of your personal information in the following situations:

items: If our processing violates laws or regulations
If we collect or use your personal information without your consent where consent is required
If you no longer use our products or services, or you have closed your account
If we no longer provide products or services to you

blocks.text: You can delete your account and all associated personal data in Settings > Account > Delete Account. After account deletion, we will stop providing services and delete your personal information as requested, unless otherwise required by law.

For additional personal information collected based on your consent, you may give or withdraw that consent at any time. After you withdraw consent, we will stop processing the corresponding personal information, but this does not affect processing that was already carried out based on your prior authorization.
You can revoke previously granted system permissions through your device settings:

items: iOS: Settings > Privacy & Security > select the relevant permission type and disable Blotz access, or Settings > Apps > Blotz > disable the relevant permission

blocks.text: After you revoke a permission, we will no longer process related personal information based on that permission, but the corresponding feature may not work properly. This will not affect other core functions.
We will respond to your reasonable requests within 15 days. If you need help, please contact us using the information at the end of this Policy.

sections.title: How We Handle Minors' Personal Information

blocks.text: Our products and services are mainly intended for adults. If you are under 18 years old, please read this Policy with your guardian and use our services only after obtaining your guardian's consent.
If we discover that we collected a minor's personal information without verifiable guardian consent in advance, we will try to delete the relevant data as soon as possible.

This Privacy Policy may be updated from time to time. You should review it periodically to understand how we protect the personal information we collect. Your continued use of Blotz services constitutes your acceptance of this Privacy Policy and any updated versions.
For material changes, we may also provide more prominent notice explaining the specific changes. Material changes include, but are not limited to:

items: Major changes to our service model, such as the purpose, type, or method of processing personal information
Major changes in our ownership structure or organizational structure
Major changes to the main recipients of personal information sharing, transfer, or public disclosure
Major changes to your rights relating to personal information processing and how those rights are exercised
Changes to the department responsible for personal information security, contact methods, or complaint channels

blocks.text: If you have any questions, comments, or suggestions about this Privacy Policy, please contact us at:
We will respond within 15 days after receiving your feedback.
